root_text.an5.206:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāmesu avītarāgo hoti avigatacchando avigatapemo avigatapipāso avigatapariḷāho avigatataṇho.
root_text.an5.206:1.4: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāmesu avītarāgo hoti avigatacchando avigatapemo avigatapipāso avigatapariḷāho avigatataṇho, tassa cittaṁ na namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya.
root_text.an5.206:1.5: Yassa cittaṁ na namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya, ayaṁ paṭhamo cetasovinibandho.
root_text.an5.206:2.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye avītarāgo hoti …pe…
root_text.an5.206:2.3: yāvadatthaṁ udarāvadehakaṁ bhuñjitvā seyyasukhaṁ passasukhaṁ middhasukhaṁ anuyutto viharati …pe…
root_text.an5.206:2.4: aññataraṁ devanikāyaṁ paṇidhāya brahmacariyaṁ carati: ‘imināhaṁ sīlena vā vatena vā tapena vā brahmacariyena vā devo vā bhavissāmi devaññataro vā’ti.
root_text.an5.206:2.5: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aññataraṁ devanikāyaṁ paṇidhāya brahmacariyaṁ carati: ‘imināhaṁ sīlena vā vatena vā tapena vā brahmacariyena vā devo vā bhavissāmi devaññataro vā’ti, tassa cittaṁ na namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya.
root_text.an5.206:2.6: Yassa cittaṁ na namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya, ayaṁ pañcamo cetasovinibandho.

translation_text.an5.206:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these five shackles of the heart.
translation_text.an5.206:1.3: Firstly, a mendicant isn’t free of greed, desire, fondness, thirst, passion, and craving for sensual pleasures.
translation_text.an5.206:1.4: This being so, their mind doesn’t incline toward keenness, commitment, persistence, and striving.
translation_text.an5.206:1.5: This is the first shackle of the heart.
translation_text.an5.206:2.1: Furthermore, a mendicant isn’t free of greed for the body …
translation_text.an5.206:2.2: They’re not free of greed for form …
translation_text.an5.206:2.3: They eat as much as they like until their belly is full, then indulge in the pleasures of sleeping, lying down, and drowsing …
translation_text.an5.206:2.4: They lead the spiritual life hoping to be reborn in one of the orders of gods, thinking: ‘By this precept or observance or fervent austerity or spiritual practice, may I become one of the gods!’
translation_text.an5.206:2.5: This being so, their mind doesn’t incline toward keenness, commitment, persistence, and striving.
translation_text.an5.206:2.6: This is the fifth shackle of the heart.
translation_text.an5.206:2.7: These are the five shackles of the heart.”
